name: z3 DPI Proof: Data Processing Inequality as UNSAT

pytorch.reason: not needed -- no autograd layer in proof sim

pyg.reason: not needed -- no graph layer in proof sim

z3.reason: Core proof engine for all three proofs. Proof 1: relative entropy DPI UNSAT. Proof 2: mutual information DPI UNSAT. Proof 3: non-CPTP negative control SAT.

cvc5.reason: not needed -- SyGuS not required for this DPI proof

sympy.reason: Symbolic derivation of contraction bound and DPI structure used to construct the z3 constraint encoding.

clifford.reason: not needed -- no geometric algebra layer here

geomstats.reason: not needed -- no manifold geometry layer here

e3nn.reason: not needed -- no equivariant network layer here

rustworkx.reason: not needed -- no dependency graph here

xgi.reason: not needed -- no hypergraph layer here

toponetx.reason: not needed -- no cell complex here

gudhi.reason: not needed -- no persistent homology here

proof1_relative_entropy_dpi.name: relative_entropy_dpi_unsat
proof1_relative_entropy_dpi.interpretation: No assignment satisfies CPTP contraction AND D_after > D_before. DPI confirmed: relative entropy cannot increase under CPTP channels.

proof2_mutual_information_dpi.name: mutual_information_dpi_unsat
proof2_mutual_information_dpi.interpretation: No assignment satisfies local CPTP constraints AND I_after > I_before. MI DPI confirmed: local CPTP cannot increase mutual information.

sympy_derivation.note: DPI for relative entropy: D_after <= lambda*D_before where lambda in [0,1] for any CPTP channel. DPI for MI: I_after <= I_before for local CPTP.

proof3_non_cptp_sat.name: non_cptp_relative_entropy_increase_sat
proof3_non_cptp_sat.interpretation: Without CPTP contraction constraint, D_after > D_before is SAT. Confirms that the CPTP axiom is the critical constraint that makes DPI UNSAT. Non-CPTP maps (e.g., non-trace-preserving, non-completely-positive) can violate DPI.

proof3b_identity_channel_dpi.name: identity_channel_dpi_unsat
proof3b_identity_channel_dpi.interpretation: Identity channel (lambda=1) also satisfies DPI -- trivially D_after <= D_before AND D_after > D_before is UNSAT.

boundary1_completely_depolarizing.name: completely_depolarizing_D_after_zero
boundary1_completely_depolarizing.interpretation: lambda=0 (completely depolarizing) forces D_after=0. All states map to the same fixed point; relative entropy collapses to 0.

boundary2_identical_states.name: identical_states_D_zero
boundary2_identical_states.interpretation: D(rho||sigma)=0 iff rho=sigma. Under any CPTP channel, T(rho)=T(sigma), so D_after=0 is forced. Confirmed UNSAT for D_after > 0.

boundary3_equality_case.name: equality_case_lambda1
boundary3_equality_case.interpretation: Equality in DPI is achievable (e.g., unitary channels, reversible maps). D_after = D_before is SAT under lambda=1 contraction.
